title: luzula luzuloides subsp. cuprina
intro: Luzula luzuloides subsp. cuprina, also known as the Cuprine Woodrush, is a rare and little-studied subspecies of the Luzula luzuloides plant, a type of sedge native to certain regions of Europe. The Cuprine Woodrush has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of the plant are thought to include the alleviation of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce swelling and fight off infections. The herbal preparations of the Cuprine Woodrush can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine.
